paper_title: Paleomagnetic and Detrital Zircon Analysis of Sespe Clasts

methods.description: Process for collecting Sespe gravel clasts and potential source rocks for paleomagnetic and detrital zircon analysis.

steps.description: We sampled Sespe gravel clasts from the Santa Ana and Santa Monica mountains and from Simi Valley.
steps.searchable_summary: collect Sespe gravel clasts from specified locations

steps.description: Collected several samples of potential source rocks to reproduce results from existing data.
steps.searchable_summary: collect potential source rock samples for comparison

steps.description: Following petrographic screening, 49 samples were selected for paleomagnetic analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: screen samples petrographically and select for analysis

methods.description: Procedure for analyzing the paleomagnetic properties of selected Sespe orthoquartzite clasts.

steps.description: All selected Sespe orthoquartzite clasts and the Shinumo sample were cut along their bedding planes with a non-magnetic brass blade.
steps.searchable_summary: cut samples along bedding planes with non-magnetic blade

steps.description: Sample cores were soaked in dilute HCl for up to 36 h to remove any possible fluid-related magnetic signatures.
steps.searchable_summary: soak sample cores in dilute HCl to remove magnetic signatures

steps.description: Demagnetization and paleomagnetic measurements were carried out using rock magnetometers with three-axis DC SQuID sensors.
steps.searchable_summary: perform demagnetization and measure paleomagnetic properties

methods.description: Analysis of detrital zircon spectra to discriminate source regions for Sespe clasts.

steps.description: Mineral separations and U-Pb isotopic analyses were performed for a total of 13 samples.
steps.searchable_summary: perform mineral separations and U-Pb isotopic analyses

steps.description: Zircon grains were mounted in epoxy, sanded, polished, and imaged with a SEM.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare zircon grains for SEM imaging

steps.description: Apply detrital zircon test to analyze populations of paleomagnetic inclinations.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze zircon data to determine source regions
